sections.heading: Pathway
sections.content: [{"pathway_id": "anal_canal_locoregional", "title": "Anal Canal Cancer — Locoregional Disease (± Positive Para-Aortic Lymph Nodes)", "branching_basis": ["Anatomic site (anal canal)", "Clinical stage", "Nodal involvement (including para-aortic nodes that can be included in radiation field)"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "Anal canal cancer, locoregional disease (± positive para-aortic lymph nodes that can be included in a radiation field) [ANAL-1]", "options": [{"type": "chemoradiation", "label": "Fluorouracil/mitomycin + radiation therapy (RT)", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": ["fluorouracil_mitomycin_rt"]}, {"type": "chemoradiation", "label": "Capecitabine/mitomycin + RT", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": ["capecitabine_mitomycin_rt"]}, {"type": "chemoradiation", "label": "Cisplatin/fluorouracil + RT", "preference": "other_recommended", "recommendation": "category 2B", "linked_regimen_ids": ["cisplatin_fluorouracil_rt"]}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "No adjuvant chemotherapy is recommended after primary chemoRT for non-metastatic anal canal cancer. The ACT II trial demonstrated that maintenance chemotherapy (5-FU and cisplatin) following chemoRT does not decrease the rate of disease recurrence [143]."}}]}, {"pathway_id": "perianal_early", "title": "Perianal Cancer — T1N0 Well/Moderately Differentiated or Select T2N0 (Not Involving Sphincter)", "branching_basis": ["Anatomic site (perianal)", "T stage", "Nodal status", "Histologic grade", "Sphincter involvement", "Surgical margin status"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "T1, N0 well or moderately differentiated or select T2, N0 perianal (anal margin) cancer that does not involve the sphincter [ANAL-2]", "options": [{"type": "surgery", "label": "Local excision with adequate margins (1 cm)", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "observation", "label": "Observe if margins are adequate", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "surgery", "label": "Re-excision if margins inadequate (preferred)", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "radiation", "label": "Consider local RT ± fluorouracil/mitomycin or capecitabine/mitomycin or cisplatin/fluorouracil (category 2B)", "preference": "useful_in_certain_circumstances", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": ["fluorouracil_mitomycin_rt", "capecitabine_mitomycin_rt", "cisplatin_fluorouracil_rt"]}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "Local excision is adequate for select early perianal lesions. A 1-cm margin is recommended [ANAL-A]. Many patients with T1 or selected T2 perianal cancers will have concomitant HSIL of the anal canal; it is important to look for such anal canal involvement when conservative management is being considered [MS-16]."}}]}, {"pathway_id": "perianal_advanced", "title": "Perianal Cancer — T1N0 Poorly Differentiated, T2–T4 N0, or Any T N+ (± Para-Aortic Nodes)", "branching_basis": ["Anatomic site (perianal)", "Histologic grade (poorly differentiated)", "T stage (T2–T4)", "Nodal status (N+)", "Para-aortic node involvement"], "nodes": [{"criteria": "T1, N0 poorly differentiated or T2–T4, N0 or any T, N+ perianal cancer (± positive para-aortic lymph nodes that can be included in radiation field) [ANAL-2]", "options": [{"type": "surgery", "label": "Local excision → Re-excision (preferred) if margins inadequate", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": []}, {"type": "radiation", "label": "Consider local RT ± fluorouracil/mitomycin or capecitabine/mitomycin or cisplatin/fluorouracil (category 2B)", "preference": "useful_in_certain_circumstances", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": ["fluorouracil_mitomycin_rt", "capecitabine_mitomycin_rt", "cisplatin_fluorouracil_rt"]}, {"type": "chemoradiation", "label": "Fluorouracil/mitomycin + RT", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": ["fluorouracil_mitomycin_rt"]}, {"type": "chemoradiation", "label": "Capecitabine/mitomycin + RT", "preference": "preferred", "recommendation": "category 2A", "linked_regimen_ids": ["capecitabine_mitomycin_rt"]}, {"type": "chemoradiation", "label": "Cisplatin/fluorouracil + RT", "preference": "other_recommended", "recommendation": "category 2B", "linked_regimen_ids": ["cisplatin_fluorouracil_rt"]}], "adjuvant_logic": {"applies": false, "summary": "For more advanced perianal cancers, treatment options are the same as for anal canal cancer. No adjuvant chemotherapy after chemoRT [ANAL-2, MS-17]."}}]}

flowcharts.title: Anal Canal Cancer — Locoregional Disease (± Positive Para-Aortic Lymph Nodes)

nodes.label: Anal canal cancer, locoregional disease (± positive para-aortic lymph nodes that can be included in a radiation field) [ANAL-1]

flowcharts.title: Perianal Cancer — T1N0 Well/Moderately Differentiated or Select T2N0 (Not Involving Sphincter)

nodes.label: T1, N0 well or moderately differentiated or select T2, N0 perianal (anal margin) cancer that does not involve the sphincter [ANAL-2]
Local excision with adequate margins (1 cm)
Consider local RT ± fluorouracil/mitomycin or capecitabine/mitomycin or cisplatin/fluorouracil (category 2B)

flowcharts.title: Perianal Cancer — T1N0 Poorly Differentiated, T2–T4 N0, or Any T N+ (± Para-Aortic Nodes)

nodes.label: T1, N0 poorly differentiated or T2–T4, N0 or any T, N+ perianal cancer (± positive para-aortic lymph nodes that can be included in radiation field) [ANAL-2]
Local excision → Re-excision (preferred) if margins inadequate
Consider local RT ± fluorouracil/mitomycin or capecitabine/mitomycin or cisplatin/fluorouracil (category 2B)
